description_long: This object consists of three bladed weapons: two short swords or large knives and one long spear or polearm. The blades are metallic, straight, and double-edged, with a silvery-gray finish, and each is fitted with a simple crossguard. The handles are cylindrical, appearing to be made of wood or a wood-like material, and are capped with a metallic pommel. The spear features a long, slender shaft with a pointed blade at the tip, while the swords have shorter handles and broader blades. The crossguards and pommels are a muted gold or brass color, providing a visual contrast to the steel blades and wooden handles. There are no visible engravings, patterns, or decorative elements, giving the set a utilitarian and functional appearance.
description: A set of two straight-bladed swords and one long spear, all with metallic blades, wooden handles, and simple brass crossguards and pommels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the spear and both swords, highlighting the blade shapes and crossguards.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profiles of the blades and the cylindrical handles, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the backs of the blades and handles, with crossguards and pommels visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blades and the straight alignment of the handles.
attribute_blade_type: straight, double-edged, spear point and sword blades
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical wood, simple metallic pommel, no visible grip texture
